description_long: This fence consists of a series of vertical wooden pickets with pointed tops, supported by horizontal rails and anchored into a concrete or stone base. The pickets are arranged in a straight line, forming a barrier that is both functional and visually distinct. The fence transitions from a solid, block-like section at one end, possibly representing a wall or reinforced post, to a more open, traditional picket style. The pickets are evenly spaced, and the pointed tops add a deterrent effect. The base appears to be made of a textured, gray material, likely concrete, providing stability and durability. The overall design is utilitarian, with no visible decorative elements beyond the pointed picket tops.
description: A sturdy wooden picket fence with pointed tops, anchored in a concrete base and transitioning from a solid block to open pickets. The design is functional and straightforward, with a focus on security and durability.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the fence, the transition from a solid block to open pickets, and the concrete base.
description_view_1: End-on view highlighting the pointed tops of the pickets and the depth of the concrete base.
description_view_2: Angled view displaying the L-shaped layout, the spacing of the pickets, and the solid block section.
description_view_3: Top-down view revealing the arrangement of pickets, the solid block, and the overall footprint of the fence.
